info.title: Affiliate APIs
info.description: A catalogue of the available APIs within our Affiliate Advertising offerings.

get.summary: Retrieve the Payment History and details.
get.description: Use this API to request Payment History Summary, Advertiser Payments History, and Payment Details. Other reports from the publisher dashboard can be pulled using the new reporting platform API. To request a specfic report, you'll need to submit your request with the appropriate reportid and the required parameters.

parameters.name: reportid
parameters.description:
Report ID. Use to identify which report to fetch:
  • 1: Payment history summary
  • 2: Advertiser payments history v1
  • 3: Payment details v1
  • 22: Advertiser payments history v2
  • 23: Payment details v2

parameters.name: token
parameters.description: Security token. This is not the "Bearer" token.

parameters.name: bdate
parameters.description: Start date of report. Use with Payment History Summary [1]. Reports can be run for up to 365 days (366 days in a leap year).

parameters.name: edate
parameters.description: End date of report. Use with Payment History Summary [1]. Reports can be run for up to 365 days (366 days in a leap year).

parameters.name: payid
parameters.description: Unique payment ID assigned to every payment. Retrieve from Payment History Summary [1].

parameters.name: invoiceid
parameters.description: Unique invoide ID assigned to every invoice. Retrieve from Advertiser Payments History [2,22].

parameters.name: nid
parameters.description: Filter by network ID. If not included, your response will return all networks.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Change the language of column headers in reports. (default: English).

parameters.name: locale
parameters.description: Change currency decimal format. If not included, your response will display with comma by default.

403.description: The number of requests allowed per minute has been exceeded

get.summary: Retrieve the MIDs for advertisers whose programs you haven't been approved for.
get.description: The Advertiser Search API allows users to retrieve a list of all advertisers and advertiser MIDs. Publishers can find potential new advertisers to apply a partnership with.

parameters.name: merchantname
parameters.description: Advertiser's name

403.description: The number of requests allowed per minute has been exceeded

get.summary: Returns list of coupons
get.description: Use the Coupon API to access coupons and promotional link data from partner-advertisers. Coupons and promotional links have been validated by the Rakuten Affiliate Network to ensure accuracy and credibility.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Filter by one or more category IDs. This parameter may contain one or more valid category IDs. If not included, your response will return all categories.

parameters.name: promotiontype
parameters.description: Filter by one or more promotion type IDs. This parameter may contain one or more valid promotion type IDs. If not included, your response will return all promotional types.

parameters.name: network
parameters.description: Filter by one or more network IDs. This parameter may contain one or more valid network IDs. If not included, your response will return all networks.

parameters.name: mid
parameters.description: Filter by one or more advertiser IDs. This parameter may contain one or more advertiser IDs. Use this parameter to limit your query to specific advertisers. If not included, your response will return all advertisers.

parameters.name: resultsperpage
parameters.description: Maximum number of results per page (default: 500)

parameters.name: pagenumber
parameters.description: Page number of results to retrieve (default: 1)

403.description: The number of requests allowed per minute has been exceeded

get.summary: Retrieve network ID, category ID and promotion type ID

parameters.name: promocat
parameters.description: Use "1" to retrive all network, category and promotion type IDs

get.summary: Retrieve transaction confirmations of events completed on a partner-advertiser's website.
get.description: There are no required parameters for Events API requests. If you call Events with no parameters provided, default values will be applied and you will receive your last 100 transactions by process date.

parameters.name: process_date_start
parameters.description: Start of date range. Returns transactions processed on or after this date. (YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss)

parameters.name: process_date_end
parameters.description: End of date range. Returns transactions processed on or before this date. (YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss)

parameters.name: transaction_date_start
parameters.description: Start of date range. Returns transactions that occurred on or after this date. (YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss)

parameters.name: transaction_date_end
parameters.description: End of date range. Returns transactions that occurres on or before this date. (YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results (default: 100)

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number of transactions to retrieve (Default 1)

parameters.name: currency
parameters.description: Currency of the sale amount for transactions

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Specify if the transaction type is a real time or batched

get.summary: Returns list of products on the market from existing advertiser product feeds.
get.description: This search will return a maximum of 1,000 results.

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: Use the parameter keyword to include all search terms in the result.

parameters.name: exact
parameters.description: Use the parameter exact to return results containing the search terms in the exact order.

parameters.name: one
parameters.description: Use the one parameter to return results containing at least one of the search terms.

parameters.name: none
parameters.description: Use the none parameter to return results containing none of the search terms.

parameters.name: cat
parameters.description: Filter results by primary or secondary category

parameters.name: language
parameters.description: Filter results by language

parameters.name: max
parameters.description: Maximum results per page (default: 100)

parameters.name: pagenumber
parameters.description: Page number of results to retrieve (default: 1)

parameters.name: mid
parameters.description: Advertiser ID

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort results by one of the allowed fields

parameters.name: sorttype
parameters.description: Sort results in ascending (asc) or descending (dsc) order

403.description: The number of requests allowed per minute has been exceeded

get.summary: Search or filter for advertisers in Rakuten Advertising network
get.description: The Advertiser API (v2) is an updated version of Advertiser Search API (v1.0) and allows users to retrieve information on advertisers. Publishers can also use this API to find potential new advertisers to apply a partnership with.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number of transactions to retrieve (default: 0)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results (default: 10; allows up to 200 per page)

parameters.name: ships_to
parameters.description: Countries the advertiser ships to

parameters.name: deep_links
parameters.description: Identifies if the advertiser allows deep links

parameters.name: network
parameters.description: Filter advertisers by network

403.description: The number of requests allowed per minute has been exceeded

get.summary: Conversion Journey API for Publisher
get.description: Allow Rakuten Advertising publishers to see their contributions towards a conversion

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start date (format: YYYY-MM-DD) of Conversion Transaction Date

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End date (format: YYYY-MM-DD) of Conversion Transaction Date

parameters.name: sort_by
parameters.description: Column to sort the results by (default: order_date).

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Order of sorting (asc or desc, default: asc).

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page number for pagination (default: 1).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Number of records per page (default: 1000).

200.description: Successful response with paginated contributed conversions.

400.description: Bad request due to invalid parameters.

403.description: The number of requests allowed per minute has been exceeded.

advertisername.example: Ｔ　三井書籍　神田支店(R)

offer_id.description: Offer ID associated with the offer that the transaction was commissioned on

sku_number.description: SKU for the item. Is blank if the Advertiser doesn't report it.

sale_amount.description: Amount the consumer paid for the item in the Order

commissions.description: Total base commission generated by this transaction

process_date.description: Date and time when this transaction was processed by Rakuten. Format used is EEE MMM DD YYYY HH:mm:ss ZZZZ. Time zone is defaulted to GMC+0000 (UTC).

transaction_date.description: Date and time when the Order was placed on the Advertiser's site. Format used is EEE MMM DD YYYY HH:mm:ss ZZZZ. Time zone is defaulted to GMC+0000 (UTC).

transaction_type.description: Identifies if the transaction is realtime or batch

product_name.description: Name of the item purchased or returned. This is an optional field for the Advertiser; only available if the Advertiser reports it.

u1.description: Identifies the click that the transaction originated from. This is an optional field for the Publisher; only available if the Publisher reports it.

currency.description: Currency of the sale amount for the transaction

is_event.description: Identifies if the reported entity is an Event or Transaction

commissions_list_id.description: ID of the commission list the transaction belongs to. This is an optional field for the Advertiser; only available if the Advertiser reports it.

ns1:name.example: トイザらス・ベビーザらス オンラインストア
トイザらス・ベビーザらス オンラインストア
トイザらス・ベビーザらス オンラインストア
トイザらス・ベビーザらス オンラインストア

ns1:linkName.example: 人気ランキング(Shift JISページ用）

long.example: Toys for only good and well behaved puppies

total.description: Total number of records in the response.

next.description: URL to the next page (if available).

previous.description: URL to the previous page (if available).

order_id.description: ALL OrderIDs that the requesting publishers are involved in.

order_datetime.description: Order Date Time. This is always in UTC+0.

advertiser_id.description: The MID associated with the OrderIDs that the requesting publishers are involved in.

journey_position.description: Journey position. This is either Awareness (first click), Consideration (middle clicks) or Conversion (last click).

conversion_publisher_category.description: This will be the category of the Publisher based on what is already captured on Affiliate Conversion Journey.

u1.description: (if/available) – A field the requesting Publisher appends to Rakuten Advertising's tracking process. This value may be masked depending on Advertisers' set up

Advertiser.description: An individual advertiser record emitted by the advertisers stream.

Event.description: An individual event/transaction record emitted by the events stream.

offer_id.description: Offer ID associated with the offer

process_date.description: Date and time when this transaction was processed (UTC)

transaction_date.description: Date and time when the order was placed (UTC)

transaction_type.description: Identifies if the transaction is realtime or batch

product_name.description: Name of the item purchased or returned

u1.description: Identifies the click that the transaction originated from

is_event.description: Identifies if the reported entity is an Event or Transaction

commissions_list_id.description: ID of the commission list the transaction belongs to

AdvertiserSearchMerchant.description: An individual merchant record emitted by the advertiser_search stream (parsed from XML).

Partnership.description: A flattened partnership record emitted by the partnerships stream. The nested advertiser object from the API is flattened into top-level advertiser_* fields.

status_update_datetime.description: When the partnership status last changed

apply_datetime.description: When the partnership was applied for

offers.description: URL to offers for this partnership

ContributedConversion.description: An individual contributed conversion record emitted by the publisher_contributed_conversions stream.

order_datetime.description: Order date and time in UTC+0

advertiser_id.description: The MID associated with the order

journey_position.description: Journey position: Awareness, Consideration, or Conversion

conversion_publisher_category.description: Publisher category based on Affiliate Conversion Journey

Content-Type.description: Type and encoding of content in response object.

Transfer-Encoding.description: Specifies the mechanism used to send the data.OPTIONS: Chunked, compress, deflate, gzip, identity

Connection.description: Specifies whether connection needs to stay alive or close. OPTIONS: keep-alive, close.

Access-Control-Allow-Origin.description: Indicates whether the response can be shared with requesting code from the given origin. OPTIONS: *, <origin>, null.

Access-Control-Allow-Headers.description: This header is required if the request has an Access-Control-Request-Headers header.

Access-Control-Allow-Methods.description: Specifies the method or methods allowed when accessing the resource in response to a preflight request.

Content-Encoding.description: This header is used to compress the media-type. When present, its value indicates which encodings were applied to the entity-body.

ETag.description: This header is an identifier for a specific version of a resource. It lets caches be more efficient and save bandwidth, as a web server does not need to resend a full response if the content has not changed.

Vary.description: This header determines how to match future request headers to decide whether a cached response can be used rather than requesting a fresh one from the origin server.

schema.example: This header specifies the technology used for the construction of APIs.

X-Request-Id.description: This header is specific to the client request to identify client's response identity for specific time period.(Token validity period).

X-Kong-Proxy-Latency.description: Latency is the time in milliseconds that Kong was waiting for the first byte of the upstream service response.

Pragma.description: This header is a general header which is an implementation-specific header that may have various effects along the request-response chain. It is used for backwards compatibility with HTTP/1.0 caches where the Cache-Control HTTP/1.1 header is not yet present.

Server.description: This header contains information about the software used by the origin server to handle the request.

Via.description: This header is added by proxies, both forward and reverse proxies, and can appear in the request headers and the response headers.
